title: Scaphosepalum lima
taxonomy: Scaphosepalum lima is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Scaphosepalum and the subfamily Pleurothallidinae. The genus Scaphosepalum is part of the family Orchidaceae, which contains approximately 30,000 species of orchids worldwide. This classification puts Scaphosepalum lima within the broader group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. As a member of the subfamily Pleurothallidinae, Scaphosepalum lima shares characteristics with other orchids that have similar growth habits and floral structures. The exact classification of Scaphosepalum lima is further refined through its unique combination of morphological and anatomical features.

intro: Scaphosepalum lima is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Scaphosepalum lima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Scaphosepalum lima include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
